brief: What COSMOS Found About Multivitamins and Cognition
method: Primary DOI/PMID metadata, PubMed abstracts, and accessible full texts reviewed directly; primary, secondary, exploratory, and derived outcomes separated.

sources.evidence_level: Parent COSMOS randomized double-blind 2x2 factorial trial; multivitamin cancer analysis
claims: secondary lung-cancer estimate and event counts
sources.verification_note: Primary full text reviewed 2026-07-19. Lung cancer was one of many secondary outcomes and was not multiplicity-adjusted; do not present as established prevention.
Primary design metadata and methods reviewed 2026-07-19. Women entered at age 65 or older and men at age 60 or older.

Primary full text reviewed 2026-07-19. MVM was a key secondary comparison. Type-I error was not controlled across secondary and tertiary outcomes; the age-equivalence calculation was called an imprecise yardstick.

Primary full text and Table 2 reviewed 2026-07-19. The identical p=0.62 values for MCI (HR 0.91) and probable dementia (HR 0.76) are confirmed from Table 2, not a transcription error. Probable dementia was adjudicated, not a confirmed clinical diagnosis. The study was underpowered and did not establish MCI or dementia prevention.

claims: 3960 randomized participants and 3562-person ITT analysis sample
sources.verification_note: Primary full text reviewed 2026-07-19. Web did not measure a prespecified global-cognition endpoint. The 3.1-year figure is a cross-sectional score translation, not a measured delay in decline.

sources.evidence_level: COSMOS-Clinic randomized ancillary study and prespecified within-COSMOS meta-analysis
claims: Clinic primary global-cognition CI crossed zero
sources.verification_note: Primary full text reviewed 2026-07-19. Global cognition pooled Mind and Clinic only; episodic memory pooled all three substudies. Bonferroni correction applied to two pooled coprimary outcomes, not retroactively to component-study analyses.

sources.evidence_level: Parent COSMOS randomized double-blind 2x2 factorial trial; cocoa cardiovascular analysis
sources.verification_note: Primary full text reviewed 2026-07-19. The total-CVD primary endpoint was null. Secondary outcomes were not multiplicity-adjusted and were labeled hypothesis-generating.

claims: null cocoa result on prespecified one-year immediate-recall endpoint
sources.verification_note: Primary paper reviewed 2026-07-19. Favorable low-diet-quality subgroup findings do not replace the null prespecified overall endpoint.
Primary paper reviewed 2026-07-19. Overall cognitive outcomes were null; subgroup findings were unadjusted.

sources.evidence_level: Long-term randomized Physicians' Health Study II cognitive analysis
sources.verification_note: Primary paper reviewed 2026-07-19. This is relevant randomized counterevidence but not a like-for-like replication of COSMOS because population, follow-up, and baseline cognitive assessment differed.

claims: selected serum carotenoid and alpha-tocopherol increases
sources.verification_note: Primary abstract and results reviewed 2026-07-19. Biomarker changes demonstrate exposure to selected nutrients, not comprehensive deficiency correction or mediation of cognitive effects.

sources.evidence_level: Prespecified randomized COSMOS epigenetic-clock ancillary analysis
claims: two nominally positive clocks among five
sources.verification_note: Primary paper reviewed 2026-07-19. The four-month figure selectively averages two nominal clock estimates; neither survives correction across five clocks, and no clinical aging outcome was measured.
Primary paper reviewed 2026-07-19. Metabolomic clocks and risk scores are surrogate models, not observed disease outcomes; no reported signal survived false-discovery-rate correction.

sources.evidence_level: Randomized COSMOS fracture ancillary analysis using self-reported doctor-diagnosed outcomes
claims: total and nonvertebral fracture adjusted hazard ratios
sources.verification_note: Primary full text reviewed 2026-07-19. Fractures were not centrally adjudicated. Estimates leaned weakly adverse, but multiple comparisons were not familywise-adjusted; do not claim definitive harm.
